mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces corporate borrowing costs and compresses equity discount rates, directly generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sudden increase in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals rapid labor market deterioration and collapsing consumer spending, causing immediate negative equity repricing within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold crossing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving sustained corporate earnings and positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day SPX returns in >60% of cases over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects capacity constraints and margin compression, leading to negative equity returns as growth expectations are downgraded.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods are followed by positive SPX 20-day returns in >50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading above 3.0% signals persistent inflationary pressure that forces markets to price in higher discount rates, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and consumption capacity, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth correlates with negative SPX 60-day returns in >55% of observations over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales indicates weakening consumer demand relative to output, causing immediate negative equity repricing as earnings forecasts are cut.
failure_condition: GDPC1-RSAFS divergence fails to predict negative 5-day SPX returns, with directional accuracy below 50% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production below -0.5% signals manufacturing contraction that reduces corporate profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines below -0.5% are followed by positive SPX 20-day returns in >60% of cases over a 7-year rolling window.
